mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and lower corporate profit growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates into higher short-term equity returns during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0 percent annually, or 5-day forward returns show no predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates robust economic activity and rising household income, supporting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive payroll revisions fail to correlate with positive 20-day returns, or the signal produces negative alpha in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 325.0, accelerating inflation erodes real consumer purchasing power and forces tighter monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in >60% of threshold-crossing events, or inflation spikes fail to predict equity drawdowns in walk-forward validation tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly expansion in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms synchronized demand growth across production and consumption sectors, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive accuracy when GDP growth decouples from retail sales, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in unemployment above 0.2 percentage points and payroll growth below 100,000 signals labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in >60% of triggered cases, or the threshold condition shows no statistical edge in out-of-sample validation.
